tool_input.summary: No PDS dataset directly encodes the literature-specific “7 emissivity pattern groups” and their inferred mineralogical classes; those are typically derived in published analyses from Venus surface-emissivity products. The most relevant PDS archives to reproduce/verify those groupings are Magellan radiometry-derived emissivity products (global gridded emissivity maps and orbit-by-orbit emissivity records), plus related Magellan derived radar property grids used to help interpret composition/roughness.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary PDS4 archive for Magellan-derived surface physical properties including *estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD)*. Use this to extract emissivity values over major volcanoes/coronae and then cluster into pattern groups as in the literature.

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: This is the SCVDR sub-collection containing the emissivity data files themselves (EDF). It is the most direct PDS4 entry point if you specifically need emissivity measurements to reproduce the volcano/corona emissivity-pattern groupings.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-SCVDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 version of the Surface Characteristics VDR. Provides the legacy-format SCVDR emissivity/radar-property products used in many older Venus surface-emissivity studies.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-GDR-EMISSIVITY-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 global derived emissivity map product (GDR emissivity). This is the gridded emissivity dataset commonly used for regional comparisons among volcanoes and coronae.

candidates.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Complementary PDS4 gridded summary of Magellan-derived radar scattering properties. While not itself a ‘mineralogical class’ product, GVDR parameters are often combined with emissivity to interpret surface physical state/composition when defining emissivity-pattern groups.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-GVDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 version of the Global Vector Data Record used alongside global emissivity products in Venus surface-property studies.

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

dataset.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains composite radar scattering functions derived from Magellan synthetic aperture radar, altimetry, and radiometry. Results are stored in ASCII and binary tables linked to pixels with 18.225 km resolution. Pixels are organized in sinusoidal equal area, Mercator, north polar stereographic, and south polar stereographic projections.
